# 'gs anchor list' shows the registered anchors: their branches,
# worktrees, and whether each is a root or internal anchor.

as 'Test <test@example.com>'
at '2026-03-29T14:00:00Z'

# setup
cd repo
git init
git commit --allow-empty -m 'Initial commit'
gs repo init

# With no anchors, the list says so.
gs anchor list
stderr 'No anchors'

# A root anchor (tracks the canonical trunk)...
gs anchor create $WORK/wt1 --name dev

# ...and an internal anchor stacked on a tracked branch.
git add feat1.txt
gs bc feat1 -m 'Add feat1'
gs anchor create $WORK/wt2 --name sub --anchor feat1

# From the primary worktree, both anchors are listed with their kind;
# neither is the trunk in effect here, so neither is marked current.
gs anchor list
stderr 'dev'
stderr 'root'
stderr 'sub'
stderr 'on feat1'

# Inside an anchor worktree, that worktree's anchor is marked current.
cd $WORK/wt1
gs anchor ls
stderr '\* dev'

-- repo/dummy.txt --
dummy
-- repo/feat1.txt --
feat1
